status=open
reason=Bash tool sandbox cannot start — every invocation fails with `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/...'`, including bare commands like `echo test` and `df -h`, and with `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true`. This is a host-level disk-full condition, not something fixable by editing repository files. Without a working shell I cannot run `go build`, `go test ./...`, `git add`/`git commit`, or `lefthook run pre-commit`, so I cannot safely make or verify the required code changes in cli/cmd/bead.go (migrating beadStore()/beadStatusStore() and callers at lines ~317-333, 468, 504, 1634, 1664 from *bead.Store to bead.Backend) or produce commit evidence. Retry once host disk space is reclaimed.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent
